reviews.review_msg: Very engaging lecturer and avoids repeating exactly what's in the readings so it makes lectures worth showing up to. Talks a lot about labour movements - personally I love that. As mentioned in other reviews he doesn't upload slides, which was a bit difficult, but assignments rely more on the readings than specific content of lectures.
Good lecturer, but you gotta be ready to take a ton of notes really quickly, as he doesn't post his slides, and there's a lot of them.

easy teacher. easy marks. the workload wasn't awful. He made us submit weekly questions regarding the readings. There was a midterm and a final for this course. Overall, the material for the course wasn't difficult. Matt was a good prof would take a class with him again
I went to 2 different classes in 2 separate semesters and decided not to take his course for the same reason: because he keeps yawning during his lecturing.

greatest cmns professor i've ever had at SFU. i've taken a course with him before and i thoroughly enjoy his lecturing style and his passion for what he teaches. super accessible outside class, very reasonable amount of homework and you will come out of the class engaged

I have never taken a class where we weren't given notes, and tbh, it wasn't that bad. I HAD to listen during class to take notes which kept me focused. Midterm was fair, T and F was hard but when is it not. Interesting Prof.

The content in this course is in no way boring, yet, he makes it sound dull. No slides, no recordings, and no aid during midterms and the final exam. Asked if he could go over key terms/themes and/or key theorists and was told no by the TA as well. It's a second-year course, students need some help. Do not recommend this instructor.

Difficult to follow because there are no slides. I cannot follow what's being said and when I do, I don't know which parts are important and what is filler. Having slides that tell you the key points on what's being said is supremely helpful so students can follow along.

Currently taking his class for CMNS240. The comment below does not understand that Matthew stressed the importance of listening and taking notes, rather than copying off of a lecture slide. You have to be attentive or else you will miss key details, but other than that, he seems to be very interested in the topics and is also very knowledgeable.

Does not understand that people need visuals aids to learn. CONSTANTLY stops to drink water/coffee and has no flow to his lectures that he doesn't record or posts notes/slides for. Even when the class asked him to make notes he said no. Lol. 10/10 do not recommend. Wish I could give him a 0.

class debate is a good way to understand the materials. When you engage in the debate, you will understand enough for the course, and the debate is not including in the marking. Presentations for each person is also a inspirational way to let you study hard. Well, if you followed all his steps, you should be ok for this.

I was a little bit nervous to take this class, as a lot of the materials looked really dense and a little dry, but Matthew made it really interesting and was great at breaking down the material in his lectures. His class really focused on class discussions of course concepts and materials, and he is great at facilitating group debate.
